OFF CALENDAR AS MOOT; AN AMENDED COMPLAINT WAS FILED. THE COURT NOTES THAT PLAINTIFF DID NOT STRICTLY COMPLY WITH CCP 472(B) (AS AMENDED IN 2016) BY AMENDING THE COMPLAINT PRIOR TO THE OPPOSITION DUE DATE FOR THE DEMURRER. Off Calendar Oct-21-2016 Assigned To Arbitration On Dec-05-16

DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Oct-05-2016 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances:
Attorney, Neil J. Rosenbaum of Rosenbaum & Associates (415.626.4111) appears as counsel for plaintiff/opposing party

Attorney, Jose Montalvo of the Law Offices of John A. Baird (925.945.4218) appears telephonically as counsel for defendants, The John Sloan Revocable Trust and John Sloan

Deputy City Attorney, Holly D. Coulehan of the San Francisco City Attorney's Office (415.554.3828) appears as counsel for defendant/moving party

Court acknowledges error in tentative ruling, states new findings in open forum. The cause is argued and submitted by both sides as set forth in the transcript of the certified shorthand reporter. The Court vacates the tentative ruling.

DEFENDANT CITY AND COUNTY OF SAN FRANCISCO DEMURRER TO FIRST AMENDED COMPLAINT is OVERRULED on the basis of sufficient facts have been alleged to invoke the Doctrine of Estoppel to assert the statute of limitations with regard to the six-month period. Counsel for the prevailing party, Mr. Rosenbaum is directed to submit a [proposed] written order that tracks the Court's ruling, verbatim.

Judge: Harold Kahn; Clerk: Jose Rios Merida; Reporter: Sherry L. Sawyer, CSR No. 5976, email: ssawyer@sftc.org. (D302)

CASE MANAGEMENT CONFERENCE
Off Calendar Aug-19-2016 Continued To Nov-09-16 At 10:30 A.M. In Dept 610

DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Aug-11-2016 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
No appearances.

The Court confirms the tentative ruling.

DEFENDANT CITY AND COUNTY OF SAN FRANCISCO'S DEMURRER TO PLAINTIFF'S COMPLAINT is OFF CALENDAR as moot; an amended complaint was filed. The court notes that plaintiff did not strictly comply with CCP 472(b) (as amended in 2016) by amending the complaint prior to the opposition due date for the demurrer. The court would have granted leave to amend in any event, however.

Judge: Richard Ulmer; Clerk: Jose Rios Merida; Reporter: Laura Martinez, CSR #11332, Bus. Tel: 415-551-3745, email: lmartinez@sftc.org. (D302)
